msgs.text:
Turn 133 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The three required current-turn attachments are present: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md. I did not use or substitute any File Library material.The target remains exactly the over-G isomorphism question from the problem statement: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.

The state remains unchanged from Turn 132:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate stays closed. The attached history records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphismΦ:F∼FwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.No structural subcase should be reopened under the present inputs. The uniform proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+Certificates D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The history repeatedly states the same structural reason: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite filtered solutions.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. Each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts: no independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. History-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

So the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).This is the uniform statement that closes the repetitive loop; it is not a cue to test another Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, degree-16 case, A2 -layer, t-layer, or Certificate D subcase.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 133 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 132. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN133-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
